_comment: Live + static EU regulatory rules fetched by fetch_rules.js. Consumed by assess_gaps.js to detect portfolio gaps.

rules.title: ECHA SVHC Candidate List (? substances as of 2026-06-26)
rules.summary: The SVHC Candidate List is updated twice yearly. Substances of Very High Concern above 0.1 % w/w in articles trigger REACH Article 33 communication duties. Current SVHC in this portfolio: BPA (Bisphenol A), DEHP, DBP, BBP (phthalates), PFAS_PFHxA.
rules.action_required: Check all products containing BPA, DEHP, DBP, BBP, PFAS_PFHxA against current Candidate List; communicate SVHC presence to customers/ECHA SCIP database.

scope.conditions: Applies when SVHC concentration ≥ 0.1 % w/w in any article placed on the EU market.

rules.title: RoHS — restriction of lead, mercury, cadmium, hexavalent chromium
rules.summary: EEE placed on the EU market must not contain lead >0.1 %, mercury >0.1 %, cadmium >0.01 %, hexavalent chromium >0.1 % by weight in homogeneous materials. Exemptions exist for specific medical and industrial uses but must be documented.
rules.action_required: All EU-market EEE must be below threshold or hold a valid documented exemption.

scope.conditions: Covers consumer and industrial EEE. Medical devices and monitoring equipment have transitional exemptions.

rules.title: Battery Regulation — LMT battery passport (deadline 18 Feb 2027)
rules.summary: Light-means-of-transport (LMT) batteries (e-bikes, e-scooters, etc.) placed on the EU market on or after 18 February 2027 must carry a digital battery passport with a data carrier (QR code) accessible from the battery surface.
rules.action_required: Create and register the battery passport data carrier before the deadline.

rules.title: Battery Regulation — industrial battery passport (deadline 18 Feb 2027)
rules.summary: Industrial batteries with a capacity > 2 kWh placed on the EU market on or after 18 February 2027 must carry a digital battery passport.
rules.action_required: Create and register the battery passport data carrier before the deadline.

rules.reference: Directive 2014/53/EU, amended by Delegated Regulation (EU) 2022/2380 — common charger
rules.title: RED — USB-C common charger mandatory for portable consumer electronics
rules.summary: From 28 December 2024, small portable consumer electronics (phones, tablets, cameras, headphones, portable speakers, handheld game consoles, e-readers) sold in the EU must use USB-C for wired charging. Laptops have until April 2026. Devices using micro-USB or proprietary connectors are no longer compliant.
rules.action_required: Replace micro-USB or proprietary charging ports with USB-C on all in-scope products.

scope.conditions: Consumer portable device with wired charging. connector IN ["micro_usb", "lightning", "barrel"] is non-compliant.

rules.title: RED — cybersecurity requirements (EN 18031) for internet-connected devices
rules.summary: From 1 August 2025, internet-connected radio equipment (routers, smart-home hubs, wearables, IoT devices, cameras) must meet cybersecurity requirements under EN 18031 (network protection, access control, personal data). CE declaration must cover Art. 3(3)(d/e/f).
rules.action_required: Conduct EN 18031 conformity assessment and update DoC / technical file before August 2025.

rules.title: PPWR — plastic film / single-use packaging restrictions
rules.summary: The new PPWR entered into force in February 2025. Products packaged in plastic film (single-use flexible plastic) for direct consumer sale must phase out such packaging or ensure it meets recycled-content and recyclability targets. Labelling requirements under Art. 11 apply from 2028.
rules.action_required: Audit product packaging; replace plastic film with compliant alternatives or document recycled-content compliance.

rules.title: POPs — decabromodiphenyl ether (decaBDE) restriction in EEE
rules.summary: DecaBDE (decabromodiphenyl ether) is listed in the POPs Regulation Annex I. Its manufacture, use, and placing on the market in concentrations above 10 mg/kg in articles is prohibited. EEE containing decaBDE in flame-retardant components is non-compliant and must be reformulated.
rules.action_required: Replace decaBDE flame retardants with compliant alternatives and update test reports.

scope.conditions: Product contains decaBDE above 10 mg/kg in any article.

rules.reference: Regulation (EU) 2023/1542, Article 12 — button cell child safety
rules.title: Battery Regulation — button cell compartments must be child-safe (Aug 2026)
rules.summary: From 18 August 2026, consumer appliances containing button-cell or coin batteries must have battery compartments that are not accessible to children without a tool or two simultaneous actions. Required warning labelling on product and packaging.
rules.action_required: Redesign battery compartment closure (tool/double-action lock) and add required warning label.

rules.reference: Regulation (EC) 1907/2006, Annex XVII — Entry on PFHxA group
rules.title: REACH restriction — PFHxA group (PFAS) in consumer articles
rules.summary: The PFHxA group restriction (Annex XVII Entry 68) prohibits placing on the EU market consumer articles containing PFHxA and its salts/esters above 25 ppb. Water-repellent coatings in wearables, textiles and outdoor products are the primary targets.
rules.action_required: Test water-repellent coatings/components for PFHxA; replace or obtain analytical proof below threshold.

scope.conditions: Consumer article with PFAS_PFHxA coating or component. Threshold: 25 ppb.

rules.title: REACH SVHC — Bisphenol A (BPA) communication and SCIP registration
rules.summary: Bisphenol A (BPA) is on the SVHC Candidate List. Articles containing BPA above 0.1 % w/w must be notified in the ECHA SCIP database and customers must be informed upon request. BPA is also restricted in certain thermal paper and food contact applications.
rules.action_required: Register in ECHA SCIP database; inform downstream users/consumers of BPA presence on request.

scope.conditions: BPA concentration ≥ 0.1 % w/w in any article.
